meta.prompt: Comprehend the cortex system for visualisation

system.name: cortex
system.summary: A portable second-brain library (`@kybernesis/brain-*`) — a family of ESM-only TypeScript npm packages that give an application persistent, portable memory. You feed it conversations, notes, transcripts and files; it stores them on a timeline, recalls them by meaning (not keywords), and builds a knowledge graph of people, facts and relationships in the background. Provider-pluggable by design: the kernel (brain-core) holds the algorithms; three swappable seams (Storage, Embedding, LLM) wire in concrete backends at startup. SQLite + OpenAI + Claude today; anything else tomorrow without touching the kernel. Powers the memory layer in KyberAgent Desktop (KAD) and KyberAgent Enterprise (KBDE).
system.monorepo: pnpm workspace, 7 packages versioned in lockstep (current 0.8.2)

seams.name: Storage
seams.powers: All persistence — timeline, entities, facts, sleep, vectors

seams.name: LLM
seams.powers: Relationship/fact extraction, contradiction detection, profiles, sleep reasoning

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-core
packages.what: The kernel — timeline, entity-graph, facts, vectors, retrieval, and sleep consolidation. Holds all algorithms; has no built-in backends. Consumers wire the three provider seams at startup, then call kernel methods. ✅verified
key_files: src/index.ts — public API surface (flat async exports by subsystem)
src/storage.ts — Storage DI seam (defaults to bundled SQLite; load-bearing)
src/vectors.ts — Embedding DI seam + semanticSearch (no-op when unset)
src/claude-call.ts — LLM DI seam (graceful degradation when unset)
src/entity-graph.ts — entity CRUD, mentions, typed relationships, profiles, insights, contradictions
src/fact-store.ts / fact-extractor.ts / fact-retrieval.ts / fact-contradiction.ts / fact-temporal.ts — fact lifecycle
src/hybrid-search.ts — semantic+keyword+temporal fusion via Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF k=60)
src/user-profile.ts — LLM-backed profile synthesis from facts
src/queue.ts — per-tenant single-fiber write queue (SQLite WAL single-writer)

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-contracts
packages.what: The seam definition package — Zod schemas, provider interfaces, shared constants. Every consumer depends on it; it depends only on Zod. Two-plane StorageProvider (Repositories = CRUD; Domain = business-logic SQL). Domain rows are snake_case by design (match KAD DB columns), enforced by a schema-naming guard test. ✅verified
key_files: src/storage.ts — StorageProvider interface (TimelineRepository, EntityRepository, FactRepository, SleepRepository, VectorRepository + domain: SearchQueries, FactRetrievalQueries, SleepMaintenance)
src/constants.ts — enums (Tier, EventType, FactCategory, EntityType, RelationshipType…), SOURCE_CONFIDENCE, EMBEDDING_MODEL/DIM (1536)/INPUT_CAP (8192), RRF_K=60, CLAUDE_MODEL_ALIASES
src/entity.ts / fact.ts / sleep.ts / timeline.ts — Zod row schemas
src/tenant.ts — TenantContext + TenantPaths + pathsFor(slug, root, brainSubdir) helper
data_shapes: Entity / EntityMention / EntityRelation / EntityProfile / EntityInsight / Contradiction

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-storage-sqlite
packages.what: Default StorageProvider — a better-sqlite3 backend persisting timeline, entity-graph, facts and sleep across separate SQLite files per tenant; bundles the vector store (lazy-loaded from brain-storage-vec). Per-slug per-kind connection pooling. ✅verified
key_files: src/provider.ts — StorageProvider factory; lazy-loads vector store
src/sleep-store.ts / sleep-maintenance.ts — SleepRepository + maintenance domain queries

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-storage-vec
packages.what: VectorRepository seam — a sqlite-vec backend for vector persistence and brute-force semantic search (vec_distance_l2). 1536-dim, fixed. Lazy-loaded by the SQLite provider; degrades to no-op if the native binary can't load. ✅verified
key_files: src/index.ts — getVectorDb()/closeVectorDb(), connection pool, schema DDL
data_shapes: chunk_meta (rowid, ts, source, content, origin_id) + chunk_meta_origin index

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-embed-openai
packages.what: Tenant-aware EmbeddingProvider wrapping OpenAI text-embedding-3-small → 1536-dim vectors. Inference plane. Per-tenant API-key resolution (<home>/.env → process.env.OPENAI_API_KEY) with per-tenant client caching; degrades to null on missing key or failure. ✅verified
key_files: src/index.ts — embed(t, text) + key resolution + client cache

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-llm-claude
packages.what: LLMProvider invoking Claude via the local `claude` CLI as a subprocess (the reasoning seam). Never throws — resolves to null on spawn/exit/empty/timeout, with an optional onError hook. 60s timeout (SIGTERM on hang), P1-hardened. Powers fact/relationship extraction, contradiction detection, profiles and sleep reasoning. ✅verified

packages.name: @kybernesis/brain-testkit
packages.what: In-memory fakes + conformance/parity harness — proves a provider implementation conforms to the contracts. Vitest-free conformance cases; runParityHarness compares baseline vs candidate by top-N overlap (default N=10, threshold 0.8). ✅verified

databases.name: timeline.db
databases.what: Timeline events and extracted facts, with FTS5 indexes. ✅verified

databases.name: entity-graph.db
databases.what: The knowledge graph: entities, mentions, typed relationships, merges, profiles, insights, contradictions. ✅verified (7 tables)

databases.name: sleep.db
databases.what: Sleep run lifecycle, maintenance queue, memory edges (related-memory graph), telemetry, tier transitions. ✅verified (5 tables)

databases.name: vectors.db
databases.what: Embedding store owned by brain-storage-vec; only materialises when an embedding provider is wired. ✅verified (lazy-loaded). Note: a 5th file, messages.db (CLI/web session storage), is KAD-application-only and NOT part of the library.

flows.name: INGEST — storeConversation (fire-and-forget)
steps: extractRelationships(t, fullText) — LLM identifies entities + typed relationships
filterNoiseEntities(...) — strip files/UUIDs/IPs/tech-terms/pronouns
addConversationToTimeline(...) — write timeline event (dedupe by title + recent window)
segmentText(...) → indexChunk(...) — embed each ~250-char segment to vectors
findOrCreateEntity / linkEntitiesWithType — write entity nodes + mentions + typed edges
extractFactsRealtime(...) — Haiku decomposes facts (category, confidence, expiry), capped 3/call
indexChunk(fullText) — single parent embedding when text is short (no segmentation)

flows.name: RECALL — hybridSearch (RRF fusion)
steps: semanticSearch — embed query → nearest-neighbour vectors (dedupe by source_path)
metadataCandidates — FTS5 MATCH (sanitized) + title/tag/summary scoring + tier/priority boosts
temporalCandidates — regex date terms → timeline timestamp matches
entityAugmentedCandidates (optional, expandQuery) — seed matched entities → linked events
reciprocalRankFusion(lists, k=60) — sum 1/(k+rank+1) across channels per source_path
cap MAX_SEGMENTS_PER_PARENT=3 + enrichByPath (tier/priority multipliers)
filter (tier/priority/type/entity/date) + sort by hybridScore
optional haikuRerank + addRelatedMemories (sleep memory_edges, top 3)

flows.name: FACT-FIRST RECALL — factFirstSearch (4-layer)
steps: Layer 1: FTS5 fact search + semantic fact search (fact:// vectors), merge by overlap
Layer 2: seed most-mentioned entities in query → traverseEntityGraph BFS 1-hop (hop penalty 0.7/0.5/0.3) → getFactsForEntity
Layer 2.5: scene expansion (nearby facts in same conversation, 0.6x) + bridge discovery (facts connecting >=2 top entities)
Layer 3: findSupportingSegments for parent conversation of each top fact
Layer 4: assembleContext to token budget + tie-break by source priority (bridge>direct>entity>graph>scene)

flows.name: SLEEP — runSleepCycleNow (9-step, concurrency-guarded)
steps: 1 Decay — age-based priority decay + expire temporal facts + weekly confidence decay
2 Tag — AI-classify timeline events (topic/domain/priority)
3 Consolidate — merge near-duplicate events by normalized title (>=3 dupes; skip pinned)
4 Link — build memory_edges (related-memory graph) for recall enrichment
5 Tier — classify events hot/warm/archive
6 Summarize — AI summaries for events lacking them
7 Observe — detect recurring facts / contradictions / temporal trends
8 Profile — AI entity narrative profiles → entity_profiles
9 Entity-hygiene — orphan cleanup, pattern/noise deletion, same-name + variant-name merges, low-value prune, profile gen

flows.name: WATCHED-FOLDER INGESTION (daemon, KAD/KBDE)
steps: startWatchedFoldersScheduler — 60s sweep, per-agent throttle + mutex, skip remote agents
runSyncCycle Pass 1 — walkDir → mtime+size diff vs state: new→storeConversation(channel=watched-folder); changed→cleanup+re-ingest
Pass 2 — deleted files → cleanupFile + drop from state
Pass 3 — unwatched folders → cleanupFile all + drop state
saveState atomically to <home>/data/watched_folders_state.json (regenerable; not SQLite/cloud)
